mechanism_of_action: The observed ultrastructural alterations, including disaxialization and margination of nuclear material, invasion of nuclear areas by cytoplasmic material, loss of nuclear material, loss of cytoplasmic granularity, increase in cytoplasmic electron density, and agglomeration of cytoplasm, are speculated to be caused by an altered intracellular ionic milieu, due to a primary effect of the antibiotic upon the plasma membrane, which is the site of selective permeability determination.
